title: Comfort Moong Khichdi
description: Soft one-pot rice and moong dal comfort bowl. Gentle on the stomach, easy to batch-cook.
explanation: Khichdi is the default recovery and comfort meal for many Indian households. Moong dal digests easily; rice gives energy; ghee is replaced here with oil or skipped for a lighter bowl.
Adjust water for porridge-soft or grainier textures. Add vegetables for a fuller one-pot dinner.
instructions: Wash rice and moong dal together until water runs clearer.
Heat oil, add cumin, hing, chilli, and ginger. Saute briefly.
Add rice, dal, turmeric, salt, and water. Bring to a boil.
Simmer covered on low heat 20–25 minutes until soft, stirring occasionally.
Adjust consistency with hot water. Finish with coriander and lemon.
stepNotes: Pressure cooker: about 3–4 whistles on medium heat with slightly less water.
More water = softer hospital-style khichdi; less water = spoonable grainy texture.
Skip chilli and ginger for a milder bowl.
Stir near the end so it does not stick.
A simple tadka of cumin and dried red chilli on top is optional but excellent.

variationSections.title: Vegetable khichdi
items: Add carrot, peas, beans, spinach, or bottle gourd with the rice and dal.
Masala khichdi: add a pinch of garam masala and tomato.
Millet khichdi: swap rice for foxtail or little millet.

variationSections.title: Serving ideas
items: Pair with plant curd, papad, and pickle for a full plate.
Top with roasted peanuts for crunch and calories.
Serve thinner as soup when appetite is low.
